result.markdown:
# hello-world

## Overview
This project is a beginner-level DevOps initiative demonstrating a sample Maven web application. It showcases CI/CD practices using Jenkins, Ansible, Docker, and Kubernetes. The web application itself is a simple registration form for DevOps learning.

[![Image](https://github.com/yankils/Simple-DevOps-Project/blob/master/Devops_course.PNG "DevOps Project - CI/CD with Jenkins Ansible Docker Kubernetes ")](https://www.udemy.com/course/valaxy-devops/?referralCode=8147A5CF4C8C7D9E253F)

## Tech Stack
| Component    | Version/Status      |
|--------------|---------------------|
| Java         | 1.7                 |
| Maven        | 3.0.3               |
| Docker       | Used for containerization |
| JSP          | Used for web application |
| Kubernetes   | Used for deployment |

## Architecture
This is a multi-module Maven project composed of 'server' and 'webapp' modules. The 'webapp' module is a Java web application built with JSP/Servlets and packaged as a WAR file. The project leverages Docker for containerization and appears to be set up for deployment using Kubernetes (indicated by 'regapp-deploy.yml' and 'regapp-service.yml').

## Entry Points
*   `Dockerfile`
*   `webapp/src/main/webapp/index.jsp`
*   `webapp/src/main/webapp/WEB-INF/web.xml`

## Directory Structure
```
.
├── Dockerfile
├── README.md
├── pom.xml
├── regapp-deploy.yml
├── regapp-service.yml
├── server
│   ├── pom.xml
│   └── src
│       ├── main
│       │   └── java
│       │       └── com
│       │           └── example
│       ├── site
│       │   └── apt
│       │       └── index.apt
│       └── test
│           └── java
│               └── com
│                   └── example
└── webapp
    ├── pom.xml
    └── src
        └── main
            └── webapp
                ├── WEB-INF
                │   └── web.xml
                └── index.jsp
```

## Getting Started
To get started with this project, follow these general steps:

### Prerequisites
*   Java Development Kit (JDK) 7 or newer
*   Apache Maven 3.0.3 or newer
*   Docker
*   Kubernetes (if deploying to a cluster)

### Build and Run
1.  **Clone the repository:**
    ```bash
    git clone https://github.com/yankils/hello-world.git
    cd hello-world
    ```
2.  **Build the Maven project:**
    Compile the web application and package it into a WAR file.
    ```bash
    mvn clean install
    ```
3.  **Build the Docker image:**
    Create a Docker image for the web application.
    ```bash
    docker build -t hello-world-app .
    ```
4.  **Deploy to Kubernetes (Optional):**
    Apply the Kubernetes deployment and service definitions to your cluster.
    ```bash
    kubectl apply -f regapp-deploy.yml
    kubectl apply -f regapp-service.yml
    ```
    You may need to adjust image names and service types based on your Kubernetes environment.

## Notes
This project is part of a larger DevOps learning course. For more details and guided instructions, refer to the associated Udemy course.

result.markdown:
# hello-world
## Overview
This repository serves as a beginner-friendly DevOps project demonstrating CI/CD principles using Docker and Kubernetes, featuring a Java web application.

## Tech Stack
| Component    | Version/Status      |
| :----------- | :------------------ |
| Java         | Backend             |
| Maven        | Build               |
| JSP          | Frontend            |
| Servlets     | Backend             |
| Docker       | Infrastructure      |
| Kubernetes   | Infrastructure      |
| JUnit        | Testing Framework   |
| Hamcrest     | Testing Framework   |
| Mockito      | Testing Framework   |

## Architecture
```mermaid
graph TD
    Maven["Maven Build System"]
    Backend["Java Backend Logic (Server Module)"]
    Frontend["JSP/Servlet Webapp (Frontend Module)"]
    DockerImage["Docker Application Image"]
    K8sDeployment["Kubernetes Deployment"]
    K8sService["Kubernetes Service"]

    Maven -->|Builds & Tests| Backend
    Maven -->|Builds & Packages| Frontend
    Frontend -->|Utilizes logic from| Backend
    Maven -->|Creates| DockerImage
    DockerImage -->|Deployed by| K8sDeployment
    K8sDeployment -->|Exposed by| K8sService
```
The project is structured as a multi-module Maven build with a 'server' module (plain Java logic) and a 'webapp' module (JSP/Servlet based frontend). The application is containerized with Docker and deployed using Kubernetes manifests.

## Entry Points
*   `webapp/src/main/webapp/index.jsp`

## Directory Structure
```
.
├── Dockerfile
├── README.md
├── pom.xml
├── regapp-deploy.yml
├── regapp-service.yml
├── server
│   ├── pom.xml
│   └── src
│       ├── main
│       │   ├── java
│       │   │   └── com
│       │   │       └── example
│       │   └── site
│       │       └── apt
│       │           └── index.apt
│       └── test
│           └── java
│               └── com
│                   └── example
└── webapp
    ├── pom.xml
    └── src
        └── main
            └── webapp
                ├── WEB-INF
                │   └── web.xml
                └── index.jsp
```

## Getting Started
To get this project up and running, follow these steps:

1.  **Prerequisites**:
    *   Java Development Kit (JDK)
    *   Apache Maven
    *   Docker
    *   Kubernetes (e.g., Minikube, Docker Desktop with Kubernetes enabled, or a remote cluster)
    *   `kubectl` command-line tool

2.  **Clone the Repository**:
    ```bash
    git clone https://github.com/yankils/hello-world.git
    cd hello-world
    ```

3.  **Build the Application**:
    Build the Maven project to generate the WAR/JAR files.
    ```bash
    mvn clean install
    ```

4.  **Build Docker Image**:
    Build the Docker image for the application.
    ```bash
    docker build -t hello-world-app .
    ```

5.  **Deploy to Kubernetes**:
    Apply the Kubernetes deployment and service manifests.
    ```bash
    kubectl apply -f regapp-deploy.yml
    kubectl apply -f regapp-service.yml
    ```
    Once deployed, you can check the service status and access the application via the exposed service URL.

## Notes
*   Specific runtime framework for the 'server' module beyond plain Java (no explicit web/API framework dependencies found).
result.mermaid_diagram:
```mermaid
graph TD
    Maven["Maven Build System"]
    Backend["Java Backend Logic (Server Module)"]
    Frontend["JSP/Servlet Webapp (Frontend Module)"]
    DockerImage["Docker Application Image"]
    K8sDeployment["Kubernetes Deployment"]
    K8sService["Kubernetes Service"]

    Maven -->|Builds & Tests| Backend
    Maven -->|Builds & Packages| Frontend
    Frontend -->|Utilizes logic from| Backend
    Maven -->|Creates| DockerImage
    DockerImage -->|Deployed by| K8sDeployment
    K8sDeployment -->|Exposed by| K8sService
```

result.markdown:
# hello-world
## Overview
This repository hosts a simple Java web application demonstrating DevOps practices, featuring a user registration form.

## Tech Stack
| Component | Version/Status |
|---|---|
| Java | 1.7 (source/target compiler) |
| Maven | 3.0.3+ |
| Apache Tomcat | latest (Docker base image) |
| Java Servlet API | 2.5 |
| JSP | 2.2 |
| JUnit | 4.10 (test) |
| Hamcrest | 1.2.1 (test) |
| Mockito | 1.8.5 (test) |
| Docker | Containerization |
| Kubernetes | Orchestration |

## Architecture
```mermaid
graph TD
    A[JSP User Interface]
    B[Java Web Application (WAR)]
    C[Java Server Module]
    D[Apache Tomcat Server]
    E[Application Docker Image]
    F[Kubernetes Orchestration]
    G[Maven Build System]

    G -->|builds| B
    G -->|builds| C
    A -->|part of| B
    B -->|runs on| D
    D -->|packaged in| E
    E -->|orchestrated by| F
```
The project is structured as a multi-module Maven build, comprising a 'server' module (likely for backend logic) and a 'webapp' module (containing JSP/Servlet frontend components). The application is containerized using Docker, with a `Dockerfile` that uses Tomcat as a base image and deploys the WAR file. Deployment and service definitions are managed through Kubernetes YAML files (`regapp-deploy.yml`, `regapp-service.yml`). The frontend provides a user registration form implemented with JSP.

## Entry Points
*   `webapp/src/main/webapp/index.jsp`: The main user interface for registration.
*   `Dockerfile`: Defines how the application is containerized.

## Directory Structure
The project has a multi-module Maven structure:
```
.
├── Dockerfile
├── README.md
├── pom.xml
├── regapp-deploy.yml
├── regapp-service.yml
├── server/
│   ├── pom.xml
│   └── src/
├── webapp/
│   ├── pom.xml
│   └── src/
└── ... (additional subdirectories)
```
Summary of key directories:
*   `.`: Root directory containing Dockerfile, main `pom.xml`, and Kubernetes deployment files.
*   `server/`: Maven module, likely for backend components.
*   `webapp/`: Maven module for the web application, including JSP and `web.xml`.
*   `server/src/main/java`: Source directory for Java code in the server module.
*   `webapp/src/main/webapp`: Contains web application resources like `index.jsp` and `WEB-INF/web.xml`.

## Getting Started
To get started with this project, follow these general steps:

1.  **Clone the repository:**
    ```bash
    git clone https://github.com/yankils/hello-world.git
    cd hello-world
    ```
2.  **Build the project with Maven:**
    Ensure you have Maven 3.0.3 or higher and Java 1.7+ installed.
    ```bash
    mvn clean install
    ```
    This will build the `server` and `webapp` modules and create the necessary `.war` files.
3.  **Build the Docker image:**
    The `Dockerfile` is at the root of the project. This command will create a Docker image with the application deployed on Tomcat.
    ```bash
    docker build -t hello-world-app .
    ```
4.  **Deploy to Kubernetes:**
    Apply the provided Kubernetes deployment and service YAML files.
    ```bash
    kubectl apply -f regapp-deploy.yml
    kubectl apply -f regapp-service.yml
    ```
    Monitor the deployment until the pods are running and the service is accessible.

## Notes
*   No explicit Java backend implementation (e.g., servlets, controllers) was found within the 'server' module or mapped in 'web.xml' to handle the `webapp/index.jsp` form submission.
*   The `index.jsp` form's `action` attribute points to `action_page.php`, suggesting an external or placeholder backend not present within this repository.
*   The specific interaction and API calls between the 'server' and 'webapp' modules are not evident from the inspected files.
result.mermaid_diagram:
```mermaid
graph TD
    A[JSP User Interface]
    B[Java Web Application (WAR)]
    C[Java Server Module]
    D[Apache Tomcat Server]
    E[Application Docker Image]
    F[Kubernetes Orchestration]
    G[Maven Build System]

    G -->|builds| B
    G -->|builds| C
    A -->|part of| B
    B -->|runs on| D
    D -->|packaged in| E
    E -->|orchestrated by| F
```

result.markdown:
# ReadmeAI
## Overview
Onboarding into a new internal codebase often means wading through READMEs that are outdated or missing altogether, plus scattered internal documentation, before you can write a single line of code.

## Tech Stack
### Backend

| Technology | Purpose |
|---|---|
| Python 3.11+ | Runtime |
| FastAPI + Uvicorn | Async HTTP server |
| Google ADK v0.3+ | Agent orchestration framework |
| Gemini (2.0 Flash) | LLM for research & writing agents |
| httpx | Async GitHub API client |
| Pydantic v2 | Request/response validation |
| slowapi | Rate limiting |
| structlog | JSON structured logging |
| OpenTelemetry | Cloud Trace observability |

### Frontend

| Technology | Purpose |
|---|---|
| React 19 | UI framework |
| Vite 8 | Build tool & dev server |
| Tailwind CSS v3.4 | Styling |
| React Router DOM v7 | Client-side routing |
| react-markdown + remark-gfm | Markdown rendering |

### Deployment

| Technology | Purpose |
|---|---|
| Docker | Containerization (Python 3.11-slim) |
| Google Cloud Run | Managed serverless hosting |
| Cloud Trace | Distributed tracing |

## Architecture
```mermaid
graph TD
    User["User"] --> Frontend["React Frontend"];
    Frontend -->|API Calls| Backend["Python Backend (FastAPI)"];
    Backend -->|Orchestrates| ResearcherAgent["Researcher Agent"];
    ResearcherAgent -->|Uses LLM| LLM["Google Gemini (LLM)"];
    ResearcherAgent -->|Fetches Repo Data| GitHubAPI["GitHub API"];
    ResearcherAgent -->|Generates Research Report| WriterAgent["Technical Writer Agent"];
    WriterAgent -->|Uses LLM| LLM;
    WriterAgent -->|Generates README| JobStore["Job Store"];
    Backend -->|Manages Jobs| JobStore;
```

The backend uses a **two-agent sequential pipeline** powered by Google's Agent Development Kit (ADK):

```
POST /api/v1/generate
        │
        ▼
  Create async job (UUID)
  Return 202 Accepted
        │
        ▼
┌─────────────────────┐
│   Researcher Agent   │  Fetches repo tree, reads config files,
│   (Gemini 2.0 Flash) │  detects stack, identifies architecture
└────────┬────────────┘
         │ JSON research report
         ▼
┌─────────────────────────┐
│  Technical Writer Agent  │  Transforms research into
│   (Gemini 2.0 Flash)    │  structured markdown README
└────────┬────────────────┘
         │
         ▼
  Store result in JobStore
        │
        ▼
GET /api/v1/generate/{job_id}
  → Poll until completed
```

**Job lifecycle:** `queued` → `processing` → `completed` | `failed`

## Entry Points
*   `README.md`
*   `back-end-cloud-run/README.md`
*   `back-end-cloud-run/pyproject.toml`
*   `back-end-cloud-run/requirements.txt`
*   `back-end-cloud-run/src/main.py`
*   `frontend/README.md`
*   `frontend/package.json`
*   `frontend/src/main.jsx`
*   `main.py`
*   `package.json`
*   `pyproject.toml`
*   `requirements.txt`
*   `src/main.py`

## Directory Structure
```
google-hackathon/
├── src/                            # Python backend
│   ├── main.py                     # FastAPI app entry point & middleware
│   ├── config.py                   # Environment config (Pydantic Settings)
│   ├── api/
│   │   ├── routes/
│   │   │   ├── generate.py         # POST /api/v1/generate & GET /api/v1/generate/{job_id}
│   │   │   └── health.py           # GET /api/v1/health
│   │   └── errors.py               # API error handling
│   ├── adk/
│   │   ├── orchestrator.py         # SequentialAgent pipeline (Researcher → Writer)
│   │   ├── agents/
│   │   │   ├── researcher.py       # Analyzes repo structure & tech stack
│   │   │   └── technical_writer.py # Produces polished markdown README
│   │   └── tools/
│   │       └── github_tool.py      # GitHub API wrapper (fetch tree, read files)
│   ├── models/
│   │   └── schemas.py              # Pydantic request/response models
│   ├── storage/
│   │   └── job_store.py            # In-memory job store + JSON snapshot persistence
│   └── utils/
│       ├── logger.py               # structlog config
│       └── tracing.py              # OpenTelemetry / Cloud Trace hooks
│
├── frontend/                       # React frontend
│   ├── src/
│   │   ├── App.jsx                 # Router setup
│   │   ├── pages/
│   │   │   ├── HomePage.jsx        # Landing page with repo URL input
│   │   │   └── ResultPage.jsx      # Generated README display with sidebar & preview
│   │   ├── components/
│   │   │   ├── Header.jsx          # Sticky navigation
│   │   │   ├── RepoInput.jsx       # GitHub URL input form
│   │   │   ├── ReadmePreview.jsx   # Preview / Raw markdown toggle
│   │   │   ├── ReadmeSidebar.jsx   # Auto-generated table of contents
│   │   │   ├── FeedbackForm.jsx    # Rating & comments
│   │   │   └── SummaryCard.jsx     # Info cards
│   │   └── services/
│   │       └── api.js              # Backend API client + mock data support
│   └── package.json
│
├── tests/                          # Backend test suite
├── Dockerfile                      # Production container image
├── Makefile                        # Build & test automation
├── requirements.txt                # Python dependencies
├── requirements-dev.txt            # Dev dependencies (pytest, ruff, mypy)
└── pyproject.toml                  # Ruff, pytest, mypy config
```

## Getting Started
### Prerequisites

-   Python 3.11+
-   Node.js 18+
-   A [Google AI API key](https://aistudio.google.com/apikey) (for Gemini)

### Backend

```bash
python3 -m venv .venv && source .venv/bin/activate
pip install -r requirements-dev.txt
cp .env.example .env   # Add your GOOGLE_API_KEY
uvicorn src.main:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000 --reload
```

### Frontend

```bash
cd frontend
npm install
npm run dev   # Runs on http://localhost:5173
```

### Docker

```bash
docker build -t readmeai:latest .
docker run --rm -p 8000:8000 --env-file .env readmeai:latest
```

## API Endpoints

| Method | Endpoint | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `POST` | `/api/v1/generate` | Submit a repo URL for README generation (returns 202 + job ID) |
| `GET` | `/api/v1/generate/{job_id}` | Poll job status and retrieve generated README |
| `GET` | `/api/v1/health` | Health check |

## Environment Variables

| Variable | Required | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|---|\
| `GOOGLE_API_KEY` | Yes | — | Gemini API key |
| `GITHUB_TOKEN` | No | — | GitHub PAT for higher rate limits |
| `GCP_PROJECT_ID` | No | — | Google Cloud project (for Cloud Trace) |
| `GEMINI_PRIMARY_MODEL` | No | `gemini-2.0-flash` | Primary LLM model |
| `GEMINI_FALLBACK_MODEL` | No | `gemini-2.0-flash` | Fallback LLM model |
| `MAX_JOB_TIMEOUT_SECONDS` | No | `120` | Max time per generation job |
| `REQUEST_RATE_LIMIT` | No | `10/minute` | API rate limit |
| `VITE_API_BASE_URL` | No | `http://localhost:8000` | Backend URL for frontend |
| `VITE_USE_MOCK` | No | `false` | Use mock data (no backend needed) |

## Testing & Linting

```bash
pytest -v                    # Run tests
ruff check src tests         # Lint
ruff format src tests        # Format
mypy src                     # Type check
```

## Cloud Run Deployment

```bash
gcloud auth application-default login
gcloud config set project <PROJECT_ID>
gcloud run deploy readmeai \
  --source . \
  --platform managed \
  --region us-central1 \
  --allow-unauthenticated
```

# ReadmeAI

## Overview
ReadmeAI analyzes public or your internal GitHub repositories to generate clear, detailed, and always up-to-date READMEs tailored for new developers today — and tomorrow can power rich documentation for other internal tools and artifacts, from Jira projects to runbooks and beyond.

## Tech Stack

| Component | Version/Status |
|---|---|
| **Backend** | |
| Python | 3.11+ |
| FastAPI | + Uvicorn (Async HTTP server) |
| Google ADK | v0.3+ (Agent orchestration framework) |
| Gemini | 2.0 Flash (LLM for research & writing agents) |
| httpx | Async GitHub API client |
| Pydantic | v2 (Request/response validation) |
| slowapi | Rate limiting |
| structlog | JSON structured logging |
| OpenTelemetry | Cloud Trace observability |
| **Frontend** | |
| React | 19 (UI framework) |
| Vite | 8 (Build tool & dev server) |
| Tailwind CSS | v3.4 (Styling) |
| React Router DOM | v7 (Client-side routing) |
| react-markdown | + remark-gfm (Markdown rendering) |
| **Deployment** | |
| Docker | Containerization (Python 3.11-slim) |
| Google Cloud Run | Managed serverless hosting |
| Cloud Trace | Distributed tracing |

## Architecture
```mermaid
graph TD
    User --> Frontend["React UI"]
    Frontend -->|API Requests| Backend["FastAPI Backend"]
    Backend -->|Manages Jobs| JobStore["Job Store"]
    Backend -->|Triggers Agent Pipeline| ADKOrchestrator["ADK Orchestrator"]
    ADKOrchestrator -->|Executes| Researcher["Researcher Agent"]
    ADKOrchestrator -->|Executes| Writer["Technical Writer Agent"]
    Researcher -->|Analyzes Repo Data| GitHubAPI["GitHub API"]
    Researcher -->|Uses LLM| GeminiLLM["Gemini LLM"]
    Writer -->|Uses LLM| GeminiLLM
    Writer -->|Stores Generated README| JobStore
    JobStore -->|Provides Job Status/Result| Backend
```
The backend employs a two-agent sequential pipeline orchestrated by Google's Agent Development Kit (ADK). Upon receiving a `POST` request to `/api/v1/generate`, an asynchronous job is created, and a 202 Accepted status is returned. The Researcher Agent (Gemini 2.0 Flash) then fetches the repository tree, reads configuration files, detects the tech stack, and identifies the architecture, producing a JSON research report. This report is passed to the Technical Writer Agent (Gemini 2.0 Flash), which transforms the research into a structured Markdown README. The final result is stored in a JobStore. Users can poll the `GET /api/v1/generate/{job_id}` endpoint to retrieve the job status and the generated README. The job lifecycle transitions through `queued`, `processing`, `completed`, or `failed`.

## Entry Points
- `README.md`
- `back-end-cloud-run/README.md`
- `frontend/README.md`
- `back-end-cloud-run/src/main.py`
- `src/main.py`
- `frontend/package.json`
- `package.json`
- `pyproject.toml`
- `back-end-cloud-run/pyproject.toml`

## Directory Structure

```
google-hackathon/
├── src/                            # Python backend
│   ├── main.py                     # FastAPI app entry point & middleware
│   ├── config.py                   # Environment config (Pydantic Settings)
│   ├── api/
│   │   ├── routes/
│   │   │   ├── generate.py         # POST /api/v1/generate & GET /api/v1/generate/{job_id}
│   │   │   └── health.py           # GET /api/v1/health
│   │   └── errors.py               # API error handling
│   ├── adk/
│   │   ├── orchestrator.py         # SequentialAgent pipeline (Researcher → Writer)
│   │   ├── agents/
│   │   │   ├── researcher.py       # Analyzes repo structure & tech stack
│   │   │   └── technical_writer.py # Produces polished markdown README
│   │   └── tools/
│   │       └── github_tool.py      # GitHub API wrapper (fetch tree, read files)
│   ├── models/
│   │   └── schemas.py              # Pydantic request/response models
│   ├── storage/
│   │   └── job_store.py            # In-memory job store + JSON snapshot persistence
│   └── utils/
│       ├── logger.py               # structlog config
│       └── tracing.py              # OpenTelemetry / Cloud Trace hooks
│
├── frontend/                       # React frontend
│   ├── src/
│   │   ├── App.jsx                 # Router setup
│   │   ├── pages/
│   │   │   ├── HomePage.jsx        # Landing page with repo URL input
│   │   │   └── ResultPage.jsx      # Generated README display with sidebar & preview
│   │   ├── components/
│   │   │   ├── Header.jsx          # Sticky navigation
│   │   │   ├── RepoInput.jsx       # GitHub URL input form
│   │   │   ├── ReadmePreview.jsx   # Preview / Raw markdown toggle
│   │   │   ├── ReadmeSidebar.jsx   # Auto-generated table of contents
│   │   │   ├── FeedbackForm.jsx    # Rating & comments
│   │   │   └── SummaryCard.jsx     # Info cards
│   │   └── services/
│   │       └── api.js              # Backend API client + mock data support
│   └── package.json
│
├── tests/                          # Backend test suite
├── Dockerfile                      # Production container image
├── Makefile                        # Build & test automation
├── requirements.txt                # Python dependencies
├── requirements-dev.txt            # Dev dependencies (pytest, ruff, mypy)
```
